title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Lyman confesses to Mrs. Oliver that she personally witnessed Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith write a codicil to her will. Mrs. Lyman explains that while she was working for the wealthy widow, she, a gardener named Jim, and a foreign girl named Miss Olga (possibly Miss Orlanda Seminova) were present when Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith wrote several lines with an ink pen and signed the document. Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith then had Mrs. Lyman and Jim sign the paper as witnesses. Mrs. Lyman observed the widow hide the signed document in an envelope inside a Victorian-era book titled *The Search for All Mysteries* on a bottom bookshelf.

Driven by curiosity, Mrs. Lyman admits she searched for the book the following day while Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith was in Manchester. She discovered the envelope and read the document, which bequeathed the widow's fortune to Miss Olga in recognition of her care during the widow's illness. Mrs. Lyman returned the papers to the book to avoid detection.

Mrs. Lyman notes that rumors later circulated claiming the codicil was a forgery. Although a lawsuit was initiated, it was dropped without a trial, and Miss Olga fled to Central Europe. Mrs. Lyman initially dismissed the situation but became unsettled following a recent murder involving a child pushed into a bucket of water. She now suspects Miss Olga may have killed Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith to secure the inheritance and has approached Mrs. Oliver for help, emphasizing that she only read the document and did not steal it.
